stem: A clinical research study measures treatment response rate across 5 trial sites, each containing multiple patient cohorts. trial site A has 5 observations with mean 60.2 % (within-trial site SD = 9.0). trial site B has 5 observations with mean 42.1 % (within-trial site SD = 3.3). trial site C has 20 observations with mean 51.4 % (within-trial site SD = 8.6). trial site D has 15 observations with mean 32.6 % (within-trial site SD = 7.2). trial site E has 15 observations with mean 41.7 % (within-trial site SD = 5.3). The between-trial site standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 5.2 and 13.0 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true treatment response rate for trial site E, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-trial site variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for trial site E: 41.8 % (shrunk from raw 41.7). Range [41.7, 41.9] as between-trial site SD varies from 5.2 to 13.0. Pooled mean: 44.2.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 44.2 % (weighted average of 5 trial site means).
Shrinkage factor for trial site E: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/15). At σ_between=8.7: B = 0.976.
Interval [41.7, 41.9] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (41.7) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (44.2) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
